def: n. m. S. समय 1. Time; period. 2. Fit or proper time. Samaë nā bāram-bār . Prov. Opportunities neglected never return. 3. Season ( rut ). 1. Kā barkhā jo krikhī sukhānī, Same chūk, phir kā pachhtānī? Bhoj. Prov. What if it rains? all dried up now the field, The season past, naught vain regrets can yield. 2. Same same kī bāt, bāj par jhapṭe bagulā . Girdhar. Each age and season hath its lay, upon the hawk the crane doth prey! 3. Tāhe samaë paṭ ḍhūṅḍan nikisīṅ, kahīṅ nahīṅ dishṭ paṛo rī, Tin meṅ ek sakhī uṭh bolī, 'dekh kadam kī orī, Chīr sab jāë dharo rī!' Sāṅore ko charitr suno rī! Holī. Sūr Dās. The maids from out the water come no where their clothes could see, When one cried out 'See there they are upon the Kadam tree!' To the nut-brown god Kanhaiya's gambols come and listen ye! 4. Noh se chhīl chhīl dāl khilāī, pānī bhar bhar piyālī, Jab paṛhne kī samaë jo āī uṛ gayā lāl, pinjrā bhayā khālī . The soul. Bhajan . Kabir. With pulse blanched daily fed, cups full of water too supplied, Just as 't was time to learn his cage he empty left and fled. same-dastkhat . A signature to an account in acknowledgment of its correctness. same same , adv. Periodically; regularly recurring. Same same sundar sabhī, rūp ku-rūp na koë . Prov. All handsome in their place, fitness the only grace.

adv. = sammukh, q.v. adv. Facing, confronting, face to face (with), &c. ( = sanmukh, the pop. form, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. samm. adj. Poisonous, venomous, baneful, infectious
more: (سمّ Poison, and فار A mouse) s. m. Arsenick, ratsbane.
subs: s.m. A large and venomous kind of lizard (the bite of which is said to produce leprosy).

def: s.m. The sable, Mustela (or Viverra) zibellina; the weasel; the marten; sable-skin; fur.
more: n. m. 1. The sable; mustela zibellina . 2. Sable-skin; fur.
s. m. 1. A weasel, a marten, a sable. 2. Sable-skin.

def: s.m. Clarified butter, ghī; fat, grease, tallow.
s.m. Encountering, confronting, meeting, opposing, facing; opposition; front, front part
more: n. m. S. सन्मुख Pr. saṅmhuṅ . 1. The front part ( āgā . 1). 2. Opposition. sāmnā k. , v. a. 1. To encounter ( āge baṛhnā , 5.). 2. To answer one; to be cheeky. Mārte kā koī sāmnā nahīṅ kartā . The man who lays about him no one will confront. sāmne , adv. Before (اَگے 1, 2, 3.). Maran chalī aur sūkh sāmne . Prov. She is going to die, Venus in the sky. sāmne kā ; A. muqābil . ( Math .) Opposite. sāmne paṛnā, yā ānā , See āge ānā , 1—4. sāmne se , adv. Before one's face or eyes. sāmne kāṭ, sanmukh kāt . ( Math .) A secant. sāmne k. , v. n. To bring (a person) before another. sāmne kī nāp (front measure), Abbr. san ; A. jaib pocket; S. भुजज्या chord of an arm, ( Math .) Sine. sāmne h. , v. a. 1. To come in front or before one; to face. 2. To brave; defy; challenge. āmne sāmne ; Sant. mohanḍā mohanḍī , adv. Face to face.
s. m. Encountering, confronting, facing, front, opposition. سامهناکرنا sāmhnā karnā, To confront, oppose, encounter.
subs: (or sāmhnā) karnā (-kā), To confront, face, encounter, oppose; to be insolent

postpn. Before, facing, in front (of, -ke, or mere, &c.), in presence (of); confronting, opposite, over against; by the side (of), in comparison (with); — sāmne-kā or sāmhne-kā, adj. (f. -ī), Confronting, opposite; of or appertaining to (one's) presence or life-time or age (e.g. sāmne-kī bāt, 'A thing said or done in (one's) presence, or during (one's) life-time')
(or sāmhne) honā (with gen.), To come in front, come forward, come before (one); to confront, oppose, brave, defy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀁𑀫𑀼𑀳 (saṃmuha), from Sanskrit सम्मुख (sam-mukhá, “facing”).

def: n. f. The heart of wheat.
postpos. or adv. In front, fronting, before, confronting, opposite.
oblique plural of شام (śām, “evening”)
oblique plural of شَمْع (śam')
vocative plural of شَمْع (śam')

vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

ety: prob. S. शम+o = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To cool warm water by mixing it with cold water, to make lukewarm, to cool; to mix.
more: v. a. S. शमन 1. To mix ( ralānā ). 2. To mix cold with warm water. Pānī samonā. samoyā-huā . 1. Lukewarm water. 2. A mixed caste; hybrid ( doglā , 1).
v. a. To cool warm water by mixing it with cool water.

ety: and سنبوسه sambūsa, q.v.; prob. = S. सम+भुज 'having equal sides'
def: s.m. A kind of puff, a kind of small pastry of minced meat of a triangular form; — a shawl, or handkerchief, &c. doubled diagonally to throw over the shoulders; anything triangular
more: n. m. 1. Small three-cornered pastry of minced meat ( tikhūṅṭ , 2). 2. A shawl, or handkerchief, etc. doubled diagonally and thrown over the shoulders.

(corrupt. for سنبوسه) s. m. 1. A kind of small pastry of minced meat of a triangular form. 2. A shawl, or handkerchief, &c. doubled diagonally to throw over the shoulders. سموسه ورقي samosa-waraḳī, A species of the dish named سموسه.
subs: s.m. A species of puff or pie with a crisp short crust, or with fine layers of crust.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سنبوسه (sanbōsa), from Middle Persian.

def: s.f. = sampat, q.v. s.m. Falling together, concurrence; falling (against or upon); descending, alighting; encountering; butting; lighting, flashing; going, moving; — name of a fabulous bird (eldest son of Garur, and eldest brother of Jaṭāya).

sect, denomination

ety: sampār˚ = S. सम्पारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+पॄ
def: v.t. To complete (a work already begun).

v.n. = sapaṛnā, q.v. v.n. To fall into, to be taken or caught, be entrapped, be seized, to become a captive; to be involved; to be comprised; — to be finished, be exhausted; — sapaṛ-jānā, intens. of, and = sapaṛnā.

s.m. Falling together, concurrence; falling (against or upon); descending, alighting; encountering; butting; lighting, flashing; going, moving; — name of a fabulous bird (eldest son of Garur, and eldest brother of Jaṭāya).

s.f. Success, prosperity, good fortune; increase of wealth or happiness, affluence, wealth, riches; a suitable state or condition; happiness; power; advancement (of any kind), advantage, benefit, blessing; completion, fulfilment, accomplishment, perfection, excellence; — sampat, A necklace of pearls, decoration, adornment; correctness, right method; excess, abundance; fate, luck; a treasure; — sam-patti, A sort of medicinal root
more: n. f. S. संपद Riches ( drab ). 1. Sukh sampat kā sab koī sāthī . Prov. All are friends of wealth and prosperity.
s. f. Affluence, wealth, riches, prosperity.
subs: adj. & s.m. Successful, prosperous, wealthy, &c.; — a prosperous or wealthy person.

def: v.n. (old H.) To arrive.
s.m. Name of a fabulous bird ( = sampāt).
n. f. S. सम्पुट A small brass cup in which offerings and chandan are placed.

s.f. = S سمپٿ सम्पुट sam-puṭ, s.m. A cavity; the closely shutting of any cavity; a box with its lid shut, a casket (syn. ḍibiyā); a small brass cup (such as is used to put offerings or ćandan in); — a kind of flower (com. called kuruvak).
s.m. A giving, bestowing; gift, donation; the person to whom a thing is given; (in gram.) the dative case.
more: n. m. 1. Gift; donation. 2. ( Gram .) The dative case (A. mafūl-i-bihyā-sānī ).

adv. Now, at this time, at present.

more: adj. S. सम्पूर्ण See purā , 1, 3, 5, 7. sampūran , adv. 1. Completely. 2. Duly. sampūran k. , v. a. See pūrā k . sampūran h. , v. n. To be finished. Kathā sampūran ho gaī .
adj. Perfect, full, completed, all, the whole.
subs: (or sampūran) karnā, v.t. To make full, to fill up; to complete, finish, &c. ( = pūrā karnā, q.v.).
